forecasts.reasoning: The resolution window (May 13 to June 14, 2025) spans 33 days. Since Trump took office, he and Zelenskyy have held at least three confirmed phone calls (Feb 12, March 19, and May 8) over roughly 108 days, representing an average interval of ~36 days between calls. However, phone calls are event-driven rather than random. The beginning of the resolution window coincides perfectly with a critical juncture in the peace process: the White House stated on May 9 that it expects Ukraine and Russia to agree to a proposed 30-day ceasefire "next week" (the week of May 12). If the ceasefire is implemented, Trump is highly likely to hold a call to claim credit and direct the next steps; if it stalls, he is likely to call to apply pressure. Furthermore, a 30-day ceasefire starting in mid-May would expire in mid-June, right at the end of the resolution window and immediately prior to the G7 Summit (June 15-17). This expiration will create another natural catalyst for high-level communication. Given Trump's preference for personal diplomacy, his active involvement in the Ukraine negotiations, and the dual catalysts of a ceasefire agreement and its subsequent expiration falling within the timeframe, there is a high probability (~75%) that they will hold at least one acknowledged phone call.
